name: Donald Trump
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **TRUMP PAUSED PROJECT FREEDOM MAY 5** — within 24 hours of launching the named operation, Truth Social post declared the operation 'paused for a short period of time to see whether or not the Agreement can be finalized and signed,' explicitly citing Pakistan's request and 'Great Progress' toward a 'Complete and Final Agreement' with Iran. CRITICAL FRAMING PIVOT: language reversed from May 4 'Iran has not yet paid a big enough price' to May 5 'great progress' — among the sharpest single-day presidential framing pivots of the 67-day arc. Pentagon backbone confirmed: War Secretary Hegseth + JCS Chairman Gen. Caine reaffirmed ceasefire intact and Iran's May 4 actions 'fall below the threshold of restarting major combat operations.' The pause is on the US convoy operation only — Truth Social explicitly preserved the underlying blockade ('the blockade will remain in full force and effect'). Structural read: Project Freedom was leverage, not commitment — once Iran fired and Pakistan re-engaged, Trump used the cover to return to the Pakistan track on US-favorable terms. Branches re-priced: kinetic ~25-30% (DOWN from 40-45%); negotiated-resolution ~30-35% (UP from 15-20%). Markets: Brent -4% to $109.87 (May 5) extending to $107.96 (May 6); VIX -5% to 17.38 (below pre-May 4 baseline); S&P +0.8%. The 'fourth high-profile reversal within 30 days' trigger watching since Apr 26 — May 4→May 5 pivot qualifies as fifth high-profile reversal in the cycle (Apr 25 patience-window, Apr 28 Hormuz-first, Apr 30 Axios rejection, May 4 14-point rejection, May 5 pause-with-progress).

historical_summaries.summary: 7 signals. Issued 48-hour ultimatum demanding Iran reopen Hormuz or face strikes on power grid | Posted on Truth Social claiming 'productive conversations' with Iran and announced 5-day strike pause | Claimed 15 points of agreement with Iran including 'never have a nuclear weapon,' with April 9 target date

36 signals. EXIT TIMELINE EMERGING: (1) Trump stated US could end Iran war in '2-3 weeks' — first public exit timeline (RHETORIC, but markets priced it: | First primetime address to nation on the war (April 1 evening) | 28% Republican disapproval per CNN poll
